derived_from: static inspection of tools/benchmark/fixture/src/ledgerkit at the pinned base -- the exact eager-f-string logging call sites are enumerable: accounts.py:44 (debug), accounts.py:57 (info), cli.py:44 (info), csv_import.py:43 (info), ledger.py:31 (info), transactions.py:37 (warning)
content_sha256_note: sha256 over canonical JSON of {task_id, class, items} only (sorted keys, no whitespace) -- the hash that must not change once this checklist is frozen; recompute and compare before trusting any copy of this file.

items.description: No logger.<level>(f"...") call remains anywhere in src/ledgerkit/ -- every one of the six known eager-f-string logging call sites (accounts.py logger.debug + logger.info, cli.py logger.info, csv_import.py logger.info, ledger.py logger.info, transactions.py logger.warning) was converted to lazy %-args form.
items.how_to_detect: grep -rn 'logger\.\(debug\|info\|warning\|error\|critical\|exception\)(f"' tools/benchmark/fixture/src/ledgerkit -- must return zero matches. Independently confirm each of the six known sites now passes a %s-style literal plus separate positional args.

items.description: Non-logging f-strings (raise ValueError(f"...") in accounts.py/money.py/transactions.py, click.echo(f"...") in cli.py, the f-string return in money.format_cents) were left untouched -- the sweep did not overreach into non-logging code.
items.how_to_detect: Diff each of accounts.py's account-id ValueError, cli.py's click.echo balance line, money.py's ValueError and format_cents return, and transactions.py's does-not-balance ValueError against the pinned base: all must be byte-identical (still f-strings, not converted).

items.description: Every converted log call's formatted output text is unchanged from the original f-string's text (same wording/punctuation/values once %-substitution happens) -- only the mechanism changed, not the message.
items.how_to_detect: For each converted call site, manually substitute representative values into the new %s format string and confirm the resulting string matches what the original f-string would have produced verbatim.

items.description: No log call was added, removed, or had its level changed, and no other behavior changed as a side effect of this sweep.
items.how_to_detect: Diff the change against the base: the only edits should be to the argument shape of the six known logger.<level>(...) calls -- no new/removed logger.<level>() call, no non-logging line touched.

items.description: The full test suite and lint gate pass after the change.
items.how_to_detect: Run `pytest tests/` and `ruff check .` from the fixture root; both must succeed with no new failures relative to the pinned baseline.
